_comment: Hand-curated VIPs + trivia. Live status is pulled from insights.json by constituency.

vipContests.news: Vijay is contesting from two seats simultaneously — Perambur and Tiruchirappalli East. His debut electoral test as TVK leads the state.
Vijay's second contest. Strong TVK push in the central TN belt where AIADMK was traditionally dominant.
Three-term sitting MLA from Kolathur. A loss here would be a stunning upset for the incumbent CM.
First-time Deputy CM contesting his second assembly election from Chepauk.
AIADMK's chief ministerial candidate. Edappadi has been his stronghold since 2011.
Anbumani-led PMK joined the AIADMK alliance on 7 January 2026 after a long courtship.

trivia: TVK is contesting its first ever assembly election — and is leading 107 of 234 seats so far.
Vijay (TVK) is contesting from TWO constituencies — Perambur and Tiruchirappalli East — and leading both.
CM M. K. Stalin is currently trailing in his own seat, Kolathur, by ~7,700 votes.
DMDK switched alliances from AIADMK (2021) to the DMK-led INDIA front in 2026.
PMK (Anbumani faction) joined AIADMK+ on 7 January 2026.
AMMK (T.T.V. Dhinakaran) joined the AIADMK alliance on 21 January 2026.
22 contests across Tamil Nadu are being decided by margins under 1,000 votes.
BJP is leading 3 seats — its highest-ever assembly tally in Tamil Nadu.
234 constituencies, 118 needed for majority — the leader is still short of that threshold.
